stories.title: Territory Days 2026 — 50th Anniversary, May 23-25 in Old Colorado City

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Territory Days turns 50 this year. 🎉

May 23-25 in Old Colorado City. Free admission, Kid Zone, 180+ booths, 25+ bands, and a gold panning area for kids.

Shuttle from downtown — parking is very limited.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you've never been to Territory Days in Old Colorado City, this is the year to change that. 🎉

Colorado Springs's beloved Memorial Day weekend festival turns 50 in 2026, and it's running May 23-25 with everything families love about it: free admission, over 180 food and craft booths, three music stages hosting 25+ bands, Wild West gunfight re-enactments, Native American dance performances, and a Kid Zone packed with inflatables, bungee trampolines, gold panning, and a train ride.

Monday afternoon features a Memorial Day Remembrance Ceremony — a really meaningful way to wrap up the long weekend with your kids.

One thing to know: street parking in Old Colorado City is extremely limited. Use the free shuttle from the Colorado Springs C.A.B. Garage downtown and save yourself the headache. VIP parking with a luxury shuttle is also available if that's more your style.

It's free. It's fun. It's been a Colorado Springs tradition for half a century. Are your families going this year?
facebook.image_caption: Territory Days turns 50 this year. 🎉 Free admission, Kid Zone, 180+ booths, three music stages — May 23-25 in Old Colorado City. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings

article.headline: Territory Days 2026: Colorado Springs's Free Family Festival Turns 50 This Memorial Day Weekend

stories.title: D49 Graduation Season — May 15 and May 23 Ceremonies

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
D49 families: graduation season is almost here.

PPEC, SSAE, and Patriot: May 15.
FHS, SCHS, and VRHS: May 23 at the World Arena.

New early childhood classrooms open at the Student Success Center in August.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D49 families, your end-of-year calendar just got a little clearer.

Graduation ceremonies for PPEC, SSAE, and Patriot are coming up on Friday, May 15. High school graduations for FHS, SCHS, and VRHS are set for Saturday, May 23, at the World Arena. If you're sending a graduate off this spring, those are the dates to lock in.

There's also been some news from the D49 Board of Education. Jack Ryan was appointed to fill the vacant Director District 2 seat, replacing Deb Schmidt who stepped down earlier this year. And the board voted to repeal Policy JBAA, which previously required students to use the bathroom corresponding to their biological sex. The policy repeal passed by vote.

On the growth side: D49 Early Childhood Education announced two new classrooms opening at the D49 Student Success Center in August 2026 — good news for families looking for early childhood options this fall.

What are D49 families most looking forward to this end of year?
facebook.image_caption: D49 families: graduation season is here. May 15 (PPEC, SSAE, Patriot) and May 23 at the World Arena (FHS, SCHS, VRHS). Plus new early childhood classrooms opening in August. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings

article.headline: D49 Graduation 2026: Ceremony Dates, Board Updates, and What's Ahead for Fall

stories.title: Pikes Peak Birding Festival + Touch-A-Truck: Two Free Family Events This Month

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Two free family events this month in Colorado Springs:

🐦 Pikes Peak Birding Festival — May 14-17, 30+ field trips and nature activities
🚛 Touch-A-Truck — May 16, 9:30 AM, free for all ages

No tickets. Just show up.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Looking for things to do with your kids this month that won't cost anything? We've got two on the calendar right now.

The Pikes Peak Birding and Nature Festival runs May 14-17 and offers more than 30 guided field trips and nature activities focused on local wildlife and conservation. It's a great way to get kids outside and curious about the natural world — and Colorado Springs is honestly one of the best places in the country for it.

Then on Saturday, May 16, the 3rd Annual Touch-A-Truck returns. It starts at 9:30 AM and it's free for all ages. Kids get up close with fire trucks, construction equipment, emergency vehicles, and more. It's one of those events where even the parents get into it.

Two free weekends. No excuses to stay home. 😄 Are your families planning to check either of these out?
facebook.image_caption: Two free May events for COS families: Pikes Peak Birding Festival (May 14-17) and Touch-A-Truck (May 16, 9:30 AM). No tickets required. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings

article.headline: Two Free Family Events in COS This May: Pikes Peak Birding Festival and Touch-A-Truck

stories.title: AAP Car Seat Guidelines — What Changed in 2026

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The AAP updated car seat guidelines in February 2026.

The old "rear-face until 2" rule is gone. Keep kids rear-facing until they hit the weight or height limit on their seat.

Better for your kid. Worth knowing.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Here's a car seat update worth knowing about.

In February 2026, the American Academy of Pediatrics updated its child passenger safety recommendations and removed the age-based guideline for rear-facing car seats. The old guidance said: keep kids rear-facing until age 2. The new guidance says: keep kids rear-facing until they hit the weight or height limit on their specific car seat — no age cutoff.

This is actually a step forward for safety. The reason the age guideline existed was to give parents a simple rule of thumb, but the real answer has always been about physical fit. Rear-facing is safer for young children in a frontal collision because the seat absorbs more crash energy across the child's entire body. Age 2 was just an approximation.

When your child does outgrow their rear-facing seat, move to a forward-facing seat with a harness and keep them there as long as the seat allows. Then use a belt-positioning booster until the lap and shoulder belt fits properly — typically when they're at least 4 feet 9 inches tall, which is often somewhere between ages 8 and 12.

Has your family been following the new rear-facing guidance, or did you hear about this update for the first time today?
facebook.image_caption: AAP updated car seat guidelines in 2026: no more "rear-face until 2." Keep kids rear-facing until they max out the height or weight limit on their specific seat. Safer for everyone. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings

article.headline: AAP Car Seat Guidelines Updated in 2026: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know

stories.title: COS Outdoor Family Guide for May — Trails, Tips, and Altitude Awareness

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
May in Colorado Springs: highs in the 60s-70s, wildflowers, and trails that are made for families.

Garden of the Gods. North Cheyenne Cañon. Seven Bridges.

Sunscreen is not optional at 6,000 feet.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
May might be the best outdoor month in Colorado Springs, and we want to make sure your family actually gets outside to enjoy it.

Temperatures typically hit the 60s and 70s during the day, mornings start cool (layers are your friend), and the wildflowers are blooming. A few family-friendly trail picks if you're not sure where to start:

**Garden of the Gods** — Free, paved and dirt trail options, dramatic red rock scenery, great for strollers and littles. Mornings are much quieter.

**North Cheyenne Cañon** — A little more rugged, beautiful canyon walls. The Seven Bridges trail is a fan favorite — just know it gets busy on weekends.

A couple of COS-specific things to keep in mind with kids:
- At roughly 6,000 feet elevation, UV exposure is meaningfully higher than at sea level. Sunscreen is a must, even on cloudy days.
- Afternoon thunderstorms are common in May — plan to head down from any exposed trail by early afternoon.

What's your family's favorite COS trail for spring? Drop it in the comments!
facebook.image_caption: May in Colorado Springs = wildflowers, 70-degree afternoons, and world-class trails right out the door. Garden of the Gods. North Cheyenne Cañon. Seven Bridges. Get outside, COS. #COSParenting #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: COS Outdoor Family Guide for May: Best Trails, Sun Safety, and Altitude Tips
